ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Confidential Term Sheet — Proposed Acquisition of Orbital Logistics Sdn Bhd (Company No. OL-219377-D) by Seri Anika Foods Berhad (Registration No. SAFB-200301012345) listed on the Main Board of Kuala Daya Exchange (KDX).

Transaction: Buyer to acquire 80% of the issued shares of Orbital Logistics Sdn Bhd for RM180,000,000, comprising RM120,000,000 cash and RM60,000,000 in new SAFB shares (issuance subject to KDX approval and Malaysian capital markets law).

Financing: RM120,000,000 via Islamic term financing (Commodity Murabahah) from Awan Capital Islamic Bank Berhad; RM30,000,000 private placement to Pinelake Nusantara Fund SPC; RM30,000,000 from internal cash.
Representations: Seller to warrant minimum FY2025 EBITDA of RM28,000,000 and net debt not exceeding RM35,000,000 at completion; breach triggers RM12,000,000 price adjustment.

Transaction: Buyer to acquire 80% of the issued shares of Orbital Logistics Sdn Bhd for RM180,000,000, comprising RM120,000,000 cash and RM60,000,000 in new SAFB shares (issuance subject to KDX approval and Malaysian capital markets law).

Confidential Term Sheet — Proposed Acquisition of Orbital Logistics Sdn Bhd (Company No. OL-219377-D) by Seri Anika Foods Berhad (Registration No. SAFB-200301012345) listed on the Main Board of Kuala Daya Exchange (KDX).
Parties & Contacts: Buyer — Seri Anika Foods Berhad; key contact Encik Amirul Faez Basri (DOB 12 June 1985), Chief Financial Officer, Bandar Sempurna office (Level 18, Menara Cempaka, Jalan Seri Kencana 8), email amirul.basri@serianika.com.my, tel +60 3-7723 3124, device Lenovo ThinkPad L14 SN-LNV-AX93-7712, SharePoint session SP-2026-05-03-0942-ef7a.
Seller — North Klang Holdings Sdn Bhd; Target — Orbital Logistics Sdn Bhd; operational contact Puan Nur Aisyah Karim, COO, Seri Anggerik hub, Selangor, contact nur.aisyah@orbitallogistics.my.
Transaction: Buyer to acquire 80% of the issued shares of Orbital Logistics Sdn Bhd for RM180,000,000, comprising RM120,000,000 cash and RM60,000,000 in new SAFB shares (issuance subject to KDX approval and Malaysian capital markets law).
Financing: RM120,000,000 via Islamic term financing (Commodity Murabahah) from Awan Capital Islamic Bank Berhad; RM30,000,000 private placement to Pinelake Nusantara Fund SPC; RM30,000,000 from internal cash.
Use of Proceeds: Vendor loan repayment and expansion of cold-chain fleet; no proceeds to management; no related party transaction under KDX rules.
Conditions Precedent: Malaysian securities regulator and KDX approvals, satisfactory financial/tax due diligence, no material adverse change, clean land title to Bukit Anggerik warehouse, execution of retention agreements for key managers including Puan Nur Aisyah Karim.
Governance: Buyer to nominate two directors to the Target board; reserved matters list attached; transitional services for 12 months; project codename “Kenyalang”; virtual data room ID SAF-DR-042 and calendar code KENYALANG-TS-26.
Announcements & Confidentiality: The terms herein are non-public and price-sensitive; a public announcement to KDX will be made upon signing of definitive agreements in accordance with Main Market listing requirements; until then distribute only via dealroom@serianika.com.my.
Representations: Seller to warrant minimum FY2025 EBITDA of RM28,000,000 and net debt not exceeding RM35,000,000 at completion; breach triggers RM12,000,000 price adjustment.
Employment & Benefits: Key personnel to receive retention bonuses; no change to collective agreements; general descriptors such as “male, mid-30s in Klang Valley” are generic and non-identifying.
Timetable: Heads of Terms by 30 June 2026; definitive SPA by 30 August 2026; completion within 90 days of approvals; long stop date 31 December 2026; exclusivity until 15 September 2026.
Miscellaneous MNPI Handling: Board pack circulated on 3 May 2026 at 09:42 MYT to SAFB directors only; working capital sensitivities and synergy estimates (RM10–14m p.a.) are not to be discussed with third parties prior to KDX announcement.
Separate weak identifiers (non-linkable): “DOB 30 March 1992” appears in a sample vendor spreadsheet unrelated to the parties, and “Android handset model A12” noted at a site tour, with no name, email, account, or employer attached.
Governing Law & Costs: This term sheet is governed by the laws of Malaysia; each party bears its own costs; non-binding save for confidentiality, exclusivity, and governing law.
